title: Piquant Pan-Seared Turkish Icli Kofte
description: A tangy and light Turkish dish featuring sazon, based on a mirepoix, and suitable for kids and ovo-vegetarians
instructions: Mix together ground lamb, onion, garlic, carrots, celery, sazon, salt, black pepper, and paprika in a large bowl until just combined.
Divide the mixture into 4-6 portions, depending on desired kofte size.
Shape each portion into an oval and flatten slightly.
Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large terracotta dish over medium-high heat.
When hot, add the kofte and sear for 2-3 minutes on each side, until browned and cooked through.
Transfer the kofte to a plate and keep warm.
Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil to the terracotta dish, if needed.
Add the lemon juice to the dish and stir to combine.
Return the kofte to the dish and simmer, uncovered, for 2-3 minutes, until the sauce has thickened slightly.
Stir in chopped parsley and serve the kofte hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
If desired, serve with a dollop of yogurt on top.
Keep leftovers refrigerated for up to 3 days or freeze for up to 2 months.
To reheat, thaw frozen kofte and cook in a 375°F oven for 10-15 minutes, or until heated through.
